---
title: "Miss White"
---

## Miss White

### Description

calling on FMY (24 Sept 1846)

### Biography

She may be the Miss White, later Mrs Gustard, dead by 1861, listed as a contributor to the <i>Hursley Magazine</i> in 1848.  The latter was perhaps Margaretta Sarah White, daughter of the Rev. John White (1761/2-1841), Vicar of Exminster, who married (Mangalore 4 December 1850) Captain H. F. Gustard, 6th Regiment of Native Infantry (<i>Morning Chronicle</i>, 22 January 1851; <i>Alumni Oxonienses</i>).  Or she may have been a member of the White family who occupied Ampfield House until at least the 1881 census, whose head in 1881 was another, different Rev. John White (b.Ampfield 1817). [Moor, <i>Erminois</i>, on Ampfield House]

### Letters

- Letter from Sept 24th [1846[[footnote:1]]]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
